Evaluation of parameters related Biochemical
TBARS examination  We examined the quantity of oxidative damage with the help of
TBARS quantity examination. TBARS is a byproduct, which was generated via lipid
peroxidation  and  their  measurement  at  the  time  of  analysis  of  thiobarbituric  acid.
We got a significant result, i.e., p < 0.001 for the analysis of TBARS amount in the
MCAO induced animals as compared to animals’ group of SHAM (Fig. 10). TBARS
content was reduced significantly [p < 0.001] when the rats contain ischemic model,
i.e., MCAO were treated with opt-P 80 MNG PLGA NPs as compared to without
treated MCAO animals. P 80 MNG PLGA NPs were restored the TBARS concen-
tration after the treatment of MCAO rats that is very close to group of animals, i.e.,
SHAM group. The animals of MCAO group were treated from P 80 MNG PLGA
NPs shows greater reduction in TBARS concentration than MNG S treated animals
